title: aristolochia oblongata subsp. calceiformis
intro: Aristolochia oblongata subsp. calceiformis, a species of flowering plant in the Aristolochiaceae family, is a type of voodoo vine or calceolaria, known for its unique, hooded flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Africa and Asia, due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Aristolochia oblongata subsp. calceiformis include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use requires caution and should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and side effects.
